title: Evangelion Endings Which is Best Which is your favorite
display_title: Evangelion Endings Which is Best Which is your favorite
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Endings - Which is Best, Which is your favorite?             Quote Post by Amalgam429 » Sat Feb 09, 2019 1:38 am Evangelion has, a lot of endings. At least more than most shows out there. From the manga, to TV, to EoE, or speculation about 3.0+1.0, NGE leaves no shortage of satisfaction (or I guess, un-satisfaction) for its fan base to chew on. But which is the best? Which is your favorite? Do you have any theories about how Evangelion endings might cris cross on each other / all happen at the same time? What do the difference in endings meeeaaannn ? Post it all here! Ps. Personally, the Manga takes the cake for my favorite ending. It just feels so, complete. If you haven't read the manga, GO DO IT! It's so worth it.

[]: Re: Evangelion Endings - Which is Best, Which is your favorite?             Quote Post by Ghdust » Sat Feb 09, 2019 11:38 pm EOE. The TV ending just takes place within the EOE ending, and in the manga ending the characters have seemingly forgotten the events of the story.

[]: Re: Evangelion Endings - Which is Best, Which is your favorite?             Quote Post by Amalgam429 » Sun Feb 10, 2019 10:35 am Ghdust wrote: EOE. The TV ending just takes place within the EOE ending, and in the manga ending the characters have seemingly forgotten the events of the story.

[]: Re: Evangelion Endings - Which is Best, Which is your favorite?             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sun Feb 10, 2019 1:41 pm As the philosopher-king of the hashishim said, none of this shit is real so just pick what you want.

[]: Re: Evangelion Endings - Which is Best, Which is your favorite?             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Feb 10, 2019 7:30 pm whynotboth.gif You know what, I've actually never watched Evangelion and just stopped with the EoTV ending. I love both for entirely different reasons, but something tells me that I would walk away feeling WAY more uplifted if I just stopped with EoTV. I should try that next time.

[]: Re: Evangelion Endings - Which is Best, Which is your favorite?             Quote Post by Amalgam429 » Mon Feb 11, 2019 10:41 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: whynotboth.gif You know what, I've actually never watched Evangelion and just stopped with the EoTV ending. I love both for entirely different reasons, but something tells me that I would walk away feeling WAY more uplifted if I just stopped with EoTV. I should try that next time.

[]: Re: Evangelion Endings - Which is Best, Which is your favorite?             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Feb 11, 2019 11:01 pm The manga take on a ending to an Eva property was interesting , and only in a way Evangelion could be. I should re-read through that just to not be interrupted by long breaks throughout and really study it. (To say that Sadamoto was never very punctual about these releases is laughable. He's been drawing these things since 1995 and only finished it a couple years ago.) But the rest of the manga before that ending was so different that I have a hard time comparing that ending to the TV series. The manga handled the characters very differently from the series, so it was arguably always a very different property heading towards a very different ending. This isn't like the TV show where Episode 1 - 24 happen, then everyone's split about which ending happens after those events. The whole story is different, so I kind of treat it differently as a result. Manga ending isn't an "Alternate Ending," it's a completely different story from front to back. The same thing goes for the "Rebuilds" too, through those don't have an ending. (Yet.)

[]: Re: Evangelion Endings - Which is Best, Which is your favorite?             Quote Post by Amalgam429 » Tue Feb 12, 2019 11:08 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: The manga take on a ending to an Eva property was interesting , and only in a way Evangelion could be. I should re-read through that just to not be interrupted by long breaks throughout and really study it. (To say that Sadamoto was never very punctual about these releases is laughable. He's been drawing these things since 1995 and only finished it a couple years ago.) But the rest of the manga before that ending was so different that I have a hard time comparing that ending to the TV series. The manga handled the characters very differently from the series, so it was arguably always a very different property heading towards a very different ending. This isn't like the TV show where Episode 1 - 24 happen, then everyone's split about which ending happens after those events. The whole story is different, so I kind of treat it differently as a result. Manga ending isn't an "Alternate Ending," it's a completely different story from front to back. The same thing goes for the "Rebuilds" too, through those don't have an ending. (Yet.)

[]: Re: Evangelion Endings - Which is Best, Which is your favorite?             Quote Post by ShonHam » Sun Feb 17, 2019 2:47 am End of Eva, by a long shot. I can appreciate an ending that is bittersweet and leaves you with more questions than answers - if done correctly that is - and EoE is a perfect example of that.

[]: Re: Evangelion Endings - Which is Best, Which is your favorite?             Quote Post by Pathetic. » Wed Apr 03, 2019 3:15 am EoTV, it always makes me smile a bit and leaves me with a sense of motivation. EoE is nice too because it's a ray of hope in what seems like complete misery.

[]: Re: Evangelion Endings - Which is Best, Which is your favorite?             Quote Post by 天使 | Nyo | 天使 » Wed Oct 02, 2019 10:15 am I think the answer is pretty obvious, right? End of Evangelion (EoE) is my favorite ending by far. It's the most real, the most possible (at least in the context of the show), and the most screwed up. I just really like how everything was just...GONE basically by the end. End of TV (EOTV) is more or less just a side ending or another "possible" ending, although it's not the one many people enjoy due to it just copping out all the buildup the series had done by that point, though visually it's actually one of my favorite parts of the whole series, I love the kind of weird, out of the box, kind of stuff they did there (though I think it was for budget reasons, I guess a limited budget can bring in some truly creative stuff...maybe even..INFINITELY creative stuff.) However, if we are talking plot, it definitely goes to EoE, it just makes the most sense and wraps up not only Shinji's Characters arc by the end, but really everyone else's as well (unlike the EOTV.) The way they went out with it all was just...AMAZING! Guess it makes sense though, Anno was basically throwing the anger from the unsatisfied fans back into their faces in the form of a movie rooted from his own frustration from those fans.

It's the most real, the most possible (at least in the context of the show), and the most screwed up. I just really like how everything was just...GONE basically by the end. End of TV (EOTV) is more or less just a side ending or another "possible" ending, although it's not the one many people enjoy due to it just copping out all the buildup the series had done by that point, though visually it's actually one of my favorite parts of the whole series, I love the kind of weird, out of the box, kind of stuff they did there (though I think it was for budget reasons, I guess a limited budget can bring in some truly creative stuff...maybe even..INFINITELY creative stuff.) However, if we are talking plot, it definitely goes to EoE, it just makes the most sense and wraps up not only Shinji's Characters arc by the end, but really everyone else's as well (unlike the EOTV.) The way they went out with it all was just...AMAZING! Guess it makes sense though, Anno was basically throwing the anger from the unsatisfied fans back into their faces in the form of a movie rooted from his own frustration from those fans.
